steps.prompt: Which words form the main verb phrase?

hints.firmatum only: 'Firmatum' is a perfect passive participle — it needs 'est' to form the perfect passive
hints.est only: 'Est' is the auxiliary — together with 'firmatum' it forms 'has been established'
hints.chaos magnum: 'Chaos magnum' is the subject — 'a great chasm'

hints.present active participle: A present active participle would be 'firmans' — 'firmatum' ends in -tum
hints.future active participle: A future active participle would be 'firmaturum' — 'firmatum' is perfect passive
hints.gerundive: A gerundive would be 'firmandum' — 'firmatum' indicates a completed action

hints.present passive indicative: Present passive would be 'firmatur' — 'firmatum est' uses the participle
hints.pluperfect passive indicative: Pluperfect passive would be 'firmatum erat' — 'est' makes this perfect
hints.future passive indicative: Future passive would be 'firmabitur' — 'firmatum est' is perfect

hints.fero, ferre, tuli, latum: These are the parts of 'ferre' (to carry/bear)
hints.fingo, fingere, finxi, fictum: These are the parts of 'fingere' (to shape/form)
hints.fio, fieri, factus sum, —: These are the parts of 'fieri' (to become/be made)

hints.accusative: 'Chaos' is indeclinable (or neuter Greek) — here it is the subject, nominative
hints.ablative: As an indeclinable noun, context determines case — here it is the subject
hints.genitive: Genitive is not indicated by context — 'chaos' is the nominative subject of 'firmatum est'

hints.nominative: 'Nos' can be nominative, but after 'inter' (between) it is accusative
hints.ablative: Ablative would be 'nobis' — 'nos' after 'inter' is accusative
hints.dative: Dative would be 'nobis' — 'nos' is accusative after the preposition

hints.nominative: 'Vos' can be nominative, but parallel with 'nos' after 'inter' it is accusative
hints.ablative: Ablative would be 'vobis' — 'vos' is accusative here
hints.dative: Dative would be 'vobis' — 'vos' is accusative, parallel to 'nos'

steps.prompt: What kind of construction is 'inter nos et vos'?

hints.prepositional phrase with ablative: 'Inter' always takes the accusative — 'nos' and 'vos' are accusative
hints.ablative absolute: An ablative absolute needs a noun + participle in ablative — this is 'inter' + accusative

translation: Between us and you a great chasm has been fixed
